attrs.alt: PIM assignment types: Eligible (no access), Active time-bounded, Active permanent, and transitions

content.text: Three Assignment States in PIM

PIM introduces a nuanced access model with three distinct states. The distinction matters because only Active assignments grant permissions — Eligible assignments are a capability to get access, not access itself.
State
Access granted?
How created
Typical duration
Use case
No assignment
None
Default for all users
Eligible
None — must activate
Admin creates PIM eligible assignment
Days to months (how long the user CAN activate)
Most privileged roles — JIT access
Active (PIM-activated)
Yes — time-bounded
User activates an eligible assignment
Hours (configured per policy, max 24h)
Current work session requiring elevated access
Active (admin-assigned)
Yes — time-bounded or permanent
Admin creates active assignment directly
Configurable; can be permanent
Contractors; break-glass; automation SPs

The key insight: Eligible = authorization to get access (not access itself). Active = actual access. Eligible assignments do NOT appear in Get-AzRoleAssignment — you must use the eligibility schedule API to see them.
What Eligible Assignments Provide and Don't Provide

Eligible assignments are intentionally invisible to standard role-listing tools. This is by design — an eligible assignment without activation provides zero access. Understanding what is and isn't included prevents incorrect security assessments.
Eligible assignment DOES provide: the ability for the user to activate the role through PIM (subject to the role's policy requirements for MFA, justification, approval).

Eligible assignment does NOT provide: any access to Azure resources or Entra directory objects. Get-AzRoleAssignment, az role assignment list, and the ARM access check API all return results as if the eligible assignment does not exist.
Eligible assignment DOES appear in: the PIM portal under 'My roles', the roleEligibilityScheduleInstances REST API response, and Entra audit logs when the assignment is created or removed.
Token behavior: a user's access token does not include the role from an eligible assignment. Only after activation does a new token (post-re-auth) include the role.
Listing All Assignment Types

A complete access inventory requires querying both the eligibility schedule API (for eligible assignments) and the standard role assignment API (for active assignments). Using only Get-AzRoleAssignment misses all eligible assignments.
Creating Eligible Assignments for Azure Resource Roles

Admin creates eligible assignments — users do not create them for themselves. The assignment grants the user the ability to activate the role, but does not grant access immediately. An optional expiry date on the eligibility itself forces periodic renewal.

# Create an eligible assignment via PowerShell (requires Az.Resources 6.x+)
# For Azure resource roles:

$params = @{
    Name                             = [System.Guid]::NewGuid().ToString()
    Scope                            = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/prod-rg'
    PrincipalId                      = (Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName 'alice@contoso.com').Id
    RoleDefinitionId                 = (Get-AzRoleDefinition -Name 'Contributor').Id
    RequestType                      = 'AdminAssign'
    ScheduleInfoStartDateTime        = Get-Date
    ExpirationDuration               = 'P180D'   # ISO 8601: 180 days eligible
    ExpirationType                   = 'AfterDuration'
    Justification                    = 'Quarterly access grant — project Blue'
}
New-AzRoleEligibilityScheduleRequest @params

# Create an active (time-bounded) PIM assignment for a contractor
$activeParams = @{
    Name                             = [System.Guid]::NewGuid().ToString()
    Scope                            = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/dev-rg'
    PrincipalId                      = (Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName 'contractor@external.com').Id
    RoleDefinitionId                 = (Get-AzRoleDefinition -Name 'Reader').Id
    RequestType                      = 'AdminAssign'
    ScheduleInfoStartDateTime        = Get-Date
    ExpirationEndDateTime            = (Get-Date).AddDays(90)   # ends in 90 days
    ExpirationType                   = 'AfterDateTime'
    Justification                    = 'Contractor engagement ending 2025-09-30'
}
New-AzRoleAssignmentScheduleRequest @activeParams
ExpirationDuration (P180D, P365D etc.) controls how long the user can activate the role — not how long each activation lasts.
Each activation duration is separately controlled by the PIM policy for the role at that scope.
If you omit ExpirationDuration and the policy allows permanent eligibility, the assignment never expires — users must be manually removed.
Eligible assignments can target users, security groups, or service principals. Group-based eligibility is a scalable alternative to per-user eligibility.
Creating Eligible Assignments for Entra Directory Roles

Entra role eligible assignments use the Microsoft Graph API through a separate endpoint. The pattern is the same — eligible means the user must activate — but the scope is the Entra directory or an Administrative Unit rather than an ARM resource.

# Create an eligible assignment for an Entra directory role via MS Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory'

$roleId = (Get-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleDefinition -Filter "displayName eq 'User Administrator'").Id
$userId = (Get-MgUser -UserId 'alice@contoso.com').Id

# Eligible assignment — user must activate; no immediate access
$eligibleRequest = @{
    action           = 'adminAssign'
    justification    = 'User admin coverage for EMEA region'
    roleDefinitionId = $roleId
    directoryScopeId = '/'     # tenant-wide; use '/administrativeUnits/<id>' for AU scope
    principalId      = $userId
    scheduleInfo     = @{
        startDateTime = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o')
        expiration    = @{ type = 'afterDuration'; duration = 'P365D' }  # eligible for 1 year
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

New-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleEligibilityScheduleRequest -BodyParameter ($eligibleRequest | ConvertFrom-Json)

# After creation, the user sees this under PIM → My roles → Microsoft Entra roles → Eligible.
# They activate it there (with MFA + justification) when they need directory admin access.
Removing Eligible Assignments

When a user should no longer be able to activate a privileged role, remove the eligible assignment. This does not immediately revoke any currently active sessions — those expire on their own schedule.

# Remove an eligible assignment (revoke eligibility)
# This prevents the user from activating the role in the future.
# Existing active sessions are NOT immediately revoked — they expire on schedule.

$scope        = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
$principalId  = (Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName 'alice@contoso.com').Id
$token        = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token

# Find the eligible schedule instance
$schedules = Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } \
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleInstances?api-version=2020-10-01&`$filter=principalId eq '$principalId'"

$scheduleId = $schedules.value[0].properties.roleEligibilityScheduleId

# Submit AdminRemove request
$removeBody = @{
    properties = @{
        principalId                    = $principalId
        roleDefinitionId               = $schedules.value[0].properties.roleDefinitionId
        requestType                    = 'AdminRemove'
        linkedRoleEligibilityScheduleId = $scheduleId
        justification                  = 'User transferred to different team'
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-RestMethod -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleEligibilityScheduleRequests/$([System.Guid]::NewGuid())?api-version=2020-10-01" \
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token"; 'Content-Type' = 'application/json' } \
    -Body $removeBody

Removing an eligible assignment while a user has an active session does NOT revoke that session. The active session expires at its configured end time. For immediate revocation of an active session, submit an AdminRemove request against the active schedule, not the eligible schedule.
Early Deactivation by the User
A user can deactivate their active PIM role before the scheduled expiry. This is a security best practice — reduce exposure time by deactivating as soon as the task is done.

# Deactivate an active PIM assignment before its natural expiry
# Useful when a user finishes their task early and wants to reduce their attack surface

$scope     = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
$token     = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token
$myId      = (Get-AzADUser -UserPrincipalName (Get-AzContext).Account.Id).Id

# Find active schedule instance for self
$active = Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } \
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignmentScheduleInstances?api-version=2020-10-01&`$filter=principalId eq '$myId' and assignmentType eq 'Activated'"

$activeScheduleId = $active.value[0].properties.roleAssignmentScheduleId

# Submit SelfDeactivate request
$deactivateBody = @{
    properties = @{
        principalId                        = $myId
        roleDefinitionId                   = $active.value[0].properties.roleDefinitionId
        requestType                        = 'SelfDeactivate'
        linkedRoleAssignmentScheduleId     = $activeScheduleId
        justification                      = 'Hotfix deployed — no longer need elevated access'
    }
} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

Invoke-RestMethod -Method PUT \
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignmentScheduleRequests/$([System.Guid]::NewGuid())?api-version=2020-10-01" \
    -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token"; 'Content-Type' = 'application/json' } \
    -Body $deactivateBody

# After deactivation, the user returns to Eligible state and can re-activate later.
# The deactivation event is logged in the Entra audit log.
Auditing Active Assignments Across Subscriptions

A cross-subscription inventory of current PIM activations answers the question 'who has elevated access right now?' This is particularly useful during an incident when you need to know who might be capable of making changes.
Detecting Permanent Assignments That Bypass PIM

In a mature PIM deployment, high-privilege roles should only be held via PIM-managed assignments. Direct permanent RBAC assignments for roles like Owner or Contributor at subscription scope are a policy violation in most security frameworks. Detect them with a cross-reference query.

# Detect active permanent assignments that bypass PIM (should be rare)
# These are NOT created through PIM activation — they are direct role assignments
# that grant access without any activation requirement.

$scope    = '/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'
$token    = (Get-AzAccessToken).Token

# PIM-activated assignments have assignmentType = 'Activated' in the schedule API.
# Direct (non-PIM) permanent assignments appear in the standard roleAssignments API
# but NOT in the roleAssignmentScheduleInstances API.

# Get all standard role assignments
$allRBACAssignments = Get-AzRoleAssignment -Scope $scope -IncludeClassicAdministrators

# Get all PIM schedule instances (both eligible and active)
$pimActive = Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $token" } \
    -Uri "https://management.azure.com$scope/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignmentScheduleInstances?api-version=2020-10-01" |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value

$pimScheduleIds = $pimActive | ForEach-Object { $_.properties.roleAssignmentId }

# Any RBAC assignment not from PIM is a direct permanent assignment
$directPermanent = $allRBACAssignments | Where-Object {
    $_.RoleAssignmentId -notin $pimScheduleIds -and
    $_.RoleDefinitionName -in @('Owner','Contributor','User Access Administrator')
}

if ($directPermanent) {
    Write-Warning 'High-privilege permanent (non-PIM) assignments found:'
    $directPermanent | Select-Object DisplayName, RoleDefinitionName, Scope
}
Azure Policy's 'Audit usage of custom RBAC roles' built-in initiative includes a policy to audit direct Owner/Contributor assignments not managed through PIM.
Defender for Cloud's 'Over-provisioned identities' recommendation flags permanent active assignments for roles that PIM policies require to be eligible-only.
Schedule this detection script to run daily via an Azure Automation runbook and alert on any findings.
Choosing Between Eligible and Active Assignments
Scenario
Recommended Assignment Type
Reasoning
Standard developer needing occasional VM access
Eligible
JIT; activate only when deploying
Break-glass emergency account
Active (permanent)
Must work even if PIM infrastructure fails
Contractor for 3-month engagement
Active (time-bounded to end of contract)
Clear expiry; no activation friction needed
Security team needing read-only view
Eligible or Active permanent (Reader)
Reader is low-risk; active can be acceptable
CI/CD service principal
Active (permanent for the duration of the pipeline)
Cannot interactively authenticate to activate
On-call engineer needing emergency write access
Eligible
Activate only during incidents; audit trail per incident
Global administrator
Eligible
Highest-risk role — JIT is mandatory
Summary
Eligible assignments grant the ability to activate a role — they provide no access until activated. Get-AzRoleAssignment does not show them.
Active assignments grant actual access. They can be PIM-activated (time-bounded, audited) or admin-assigned (direct, time-bounded or permanent).
Prefer eligible assignments for all high-privilege roles; reserve direct active assignments for break-glass accounts and automation service principals.
Use the roleEligibilityScheduleInstances and roleAssignmentScheduleInstances REST APIs for a complete inventory — standard RBAC APIs are incomplete.
Detect direct permanent assignments on sensitive roles with a cross-reference query and alert on violations.
Users can deactivate early; admins can force-deactivate via AdminRemove. Removing an eligible assignment does not immediately terminate active sessions.
